shell_workflow.overview: This workflow defines the Bob Shell oriented CVE remediation process for GitHub Actions. It is designed for non-interactive execution where the prompt already contains the target issue or repository context. The workflow must avoid user-dependent discovery patterns and must produce machine-parseable output for downstream workflow steps.

execution_model.approval_pattern: Phase 1 creates the remediation PR and then exits cleanly so GitHub Actions can pause for human approval outside of Bob. Later workflow phases are triggered by GitHub events rather than by an in-chat follow-up.
execution_model.critical_difference_from_interactive_mod: Do not behave like an IDE assistant waiting for step-by-step human approval after each action. Complete the current automation phase in one run and emit a final structured summary.

step.title: Trust the workflow-provided context
step.description: The GitHub Actions prompt should provide the issue number, repository, and expected workflow. Treat that prompt as the primary task definition instead of asking discovery questions.

actions.action: Extract the GitHub issue number or issue URL from the prompt if present
Extract repository owner and repo from the prompt, URL, or local git remote context if needed
Assume credentials are already provisioned through environment variables, git auth, and Bob Shell MCP config

step.title: Choose GitHub MCP first, then fallback safely
step.description: This shell mode is intended to make GitHub MCP work inside Bob Shell on GitHub Actions. GitHub MCP is the preferred path for issue retrieval, branch creation, PR creation, comments, labels, and reviewer requests. Use GitHub CLI or authenticated REST calls only as fallback mechanisms when MCP fails, is unavailable, or returns incomplete data.

priority_order.priority: GitHub MCP for all supported GitHub operations
Local git for repository workspace changes, commits, branch checkout, and push
GitHub CLI or authenticated REST API only if the MCP path fails or cannot complete the required action

steps.step: Fetch the target issue details using GitHub MCP as the primary mechanism
Extract CVE ID, package name, application context, and any labels or severity hints
If the issue body is incomplete, infer only from authoritative sources and document the missing data clearly
Load Concert credentials from the local environment or `.env` file
Query Concert APIs to validate the affected application and package exposure
Retrieve recommended upgrade version and risk context
Cross-check Concert findings with at least one authoritative public vulnerability source

phase.name: branch_and_manifest_update
phase.title: Create fix branch and update manifest files

steps.step: Create or checkout a remediation branch before editing files
Update only dependency manifests, lockfiles, or package metadata required for the package fix
Do not modify application code or tests in this mode
Keep changes minimal and attributable to the CVE remediation

phase.name: commit_push_and_pr
phase.title: Commit, push, and create pull request

steps.step: Commit with a structured CVE-focused message
Push the remediation branch to origin
Create the pull request using GitHub MCP as the preferred path
Add labels, reviewers, or comments using GitHub MCP when supported; use CLI/API only as fallback

phase.name: structured_completion
phase.title: Emit machine-readable completion output
phase.description: The GitHub Actions workflow needs to extract PR metadata from Bob Shell output.

output_contract.requirement: End the phase with a concise summary that includes literal fields for downstream parsing.

guidance.item: Always include both PR number and PR URL
Prefer the GitHub web URL for PR_URL
Use exact branch name pushed to origin
Keep field names stable and uppercase for easy grep-based extraction

out_of_scope.item: Running unit tests as a decision gate for this mode
Fixing application code for post-upgrade breakage
Waiting inside Bob Shell for external approval events
